title: I Don'T Wanna
lyrics:
[verse 1]
This bowl is too small for my spoon!
The milk’s gonna splash on the moon!
You said it’s not cereal—why?
I want my loops back, no lie!

[chorus]
I don’t wanna! No, no, no!
I don’t wanna! Let me go!
I don’t wanna eat this glop!
I don’t wanna—Stop!

[verse 2]
Oatmeal’s got lumps like a rock!
It’s gonna stick in my sock!
I’ll trade you my peas for a bite—
But not if they’re green. That’s not right!

[chorus]
I don’t wanna! No, no, no!
I don’t wanna! Let me go!
I don’t wanna eat this glop!
I don’t wanna—Stop!

[ending]
Fine… I’ll take one tiny bite…
Mmm… okay, it’s kinda… alright?
*snore*
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, full musical production, kalimba, bass, and stomps, 121 BPM, super bouncy, high energy, strong groove, clear warm vocal, strong singalong chorus, every word easy to understand
scene: 7:42 a.m., kitchen—you just pried the cereal box from their hands and said, "No, it's oatmeal today," as the microwave beeped behind you. Their socked feet skid on the linoleum as they wail, "I don't wanna!" and clutch the box like a life preserver.
cover_description: A tiny kid in dinosaur pajamas clings to a giant cereal box like a shield, oatmeal flying in slow-motion splats behind them, one sock lost in the chaos, eyes squeezed shut in full protest.
